name: Metformin
agent_summary: Very small, highly polar guanidine with extremely low logP and QED. Passes Lipinski, but properties suggest reliance on active transport for absorption.
agent_liabilities: High polarity / very low logP, predicting poor passive membrane permeability., Low QED value, indicating deviation from typical oral drug-likeness criteria., High basicity of the guanidine moiety, leading to full protonation at physiological pH.
agent_optimization: Increase lipophilicity through the addition of small alkyl or aryl substituents., Incorporate a small, rigid ring system to modulate MW and potentially improve QED., Explore prodrug strategies to temporarily mask the highly polar guanidine for absorption., Investigate isosteric replacements to modulate basicity and overall polarity.

name: Lopinavir
agent_summary: A large, highly polar, and flexible peptide-like molecule with very low drug-likeness, indicating potential challenges for permeability and bioavailability.
agent_liabilities: Very high molecular weight (>745 Da)., Excessive topological polar surface area (>226 Å²)., Low quantitative estimate of drug-likeness (QED: 0.141)., Multiple hydrolyzable ester groups., High number of rotatable bonds (13).
agent_optimization: Significantly reduce overall molecular weight and size., Minimize polarity, especially the free carboxylic acid and potentially reduce HBD count., Improve metabolic stability by addressing hydrolyzable ester functionalities., Introduce rigidity into the scaffold to reduce rotatable bonds.
